rationale: This benchmark tests an AI agent's ability to parse Markdown structure, identify nested subsections and their complete content including child elements, and relocate them to a different parent section. It also requires discernment to ignore mentions of the subsection title that appear within raw code blocks or quoted archival text.
task_description: In the provided Markdown document, move the complete nested subsection titled "## 3.1.2 Database Migration" from its current parent section "## 3.1 Migration Steps" to the parent section "## 3.2 Verification Steps". Ensure all paragraphs, lists, tables, and fenced code blocks belonging exclusively to the "## 3.1.2 Database Migration" subsection are moved along with it. Ignore the mention of "Database Migration" that appears inside the code block within the "## 3.1.1 Pre-migration Backups" subsection.
input_markdown:
# 3. Migration Plan

## 3.1 Migration Steps

### 3.1.1 Pre-migration Backups

Backup all critical databases and application data. This is essential.

```sql
-- Example backup script:
BACKUP DATABASE production_db;
-- Run: /opt/run_backups.sh
```

### 3.1.2 Database Migration

All database migration tasks will be executed by the database team.

- Pre-migration schema review
- Data validation checks
- Post-migration index rebuilding

| Table      | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| users     | Migrate |
| products  | Migrate |
| orders    | Migrate |

```bash
# Database Migration
echo "Starting database migration process..."
```

## 3.2 Verification Steps

### 3.2.1 Smoke Tests

Basic connectivity and functionality checks.

### 3.2.2 Data Integrity Checks

Validate data consistency across migrated tables.
expected_markdown:
# 3. Migration Plan

## 3.1 Migration Steps

### 3.1.1 Pre-migration Backups

Backup all critical databases and application data. This is essential.

```sql
-- Example backup script:
BACKUP DATABASE production_db;
-- Run: /opt/run_backups.sh
```

## 3.2 Verification Steps

### 3.2.1 Smoke Tests

Basic connectivity and functionality checks.

### 3.2.2 Data Integrity Checks

Validate data consistency across migrated tables.

### 3.1.2 Database Migration

All database migration tasks will be executed by the database team.

- Pre-migration schema review
- Data validation checks
- Post-migration index rebuilding

| Table      | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| users     | Migrate |
| products  | Migrate |
| orders    | Migrate |

```bash
# Database Migration
echo "Starting database migration process..."
```
scorer_policy: The scorer will verify that the complete content of the subsection originally under "## 3.1 Migration Steps" (namely, the "## 3.1.2 Database Migration" subsection including its paragraphs, list items, table, and fenced code block) has been moved in its entirety to become a child of "## 3.2 Verification Steps". The scorer must also confirm that the mention of "Database Migration" inside the SQL code block under "## 3.1.1 Pre-migration Backups" remains untouched and that no other content has been altered.
